diagnosis: ECG shows classic findings of acute/hyperacute anterior wall Q wave myocardial infarction (MI), with reciprocal inferior ST depressions. The distribution of changes is consistent with a proximal left anterior descending (LAD) occlusion which was confirmed at cardiac catheterization and treated with angioplasty/stent. Premature atherosclerosis here was associated with multiple risk factors for coronary artery disease:hypertension, hyperlipidemia, family history and tobacco.
